message:
fix(vdbe): guard variable-length column gather against null/invalid rows in hash join

The `gather_column_inner` function materializes output columns from the
hash table's build side after probe. For variable-length types (Text
and Blob), it reads offset pairs from the offsets array to slice into
the source data buffer. However, it was unconditionally reading offsets
even for rows marked as null (via extra_nulls) or invalid (via the
validity bitmap).

This is dangerous because null/invalid rows may have stale or
out-of-bounds indices that point outside the source data buffer. While
the existing bounds check (start <= end && end <= src.len()) prevented
actual out-of-bounds reads, it would silently copy garbage data for
null rows instead of producing empty slices, corrupting the output
column.

The fix adds an `if !extra_nulls[out_idx] && validity.is_valid(row)`
guard before accessing the offsets array. For null/invalid rows, we
skip the data copy entirely and just push the current offset (producing
a zero-length slice), which is the correct representation of NULL in
the variable-length column format. The offset accounting remains
correct because new_offsets always gets a push regardless.

Applied identically to both the Text and Blob branches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
